message:
fix(fcp-crypto): reject CBOR tags in deterministic encoding

Deterministic CBOR forbids tagged values: a tag byte changes the
serialized form without surfacing any matching invariant at the
`ciborium::value::Value` layer, so two semantically-equivalent
representations (tagged vs. untagged) could re-encode to identical
bytes — breaking the "every input has exactly one canonical encoding"
guarantee that signature/hash determinism relies on.

Previously `canonicalize_value_in_place` recursed into the tag's inner
value and discarded the tag, which meant a tagged payload would
canonicalize to the same bytes as the untagged inner value. That is
not a tag-aware decision; it silently strips signal that callers may
have been depending on for domain separation.

This patch makes the canonicalizer hard-fail with
`CryptoError::SerializationError("CBOR tag <n> not allowed in
deterministic CBOR")` when a tagged value is encountered, mirroring
the rest of `canonicalize_value_in_place` (which already rejects
non-finite floats, indefinite-length encodings, and oversized maps).
A new unit test (`deterministic_cbor_rejects_tags`) pins the contract.

Doc comment for `to_deterministic_cbor` updated to list "CBOR tags
rejected" alongside the other invariants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
